title: httptest, Custom RoundTrippers, Fuzzing, Race Evidence, and Mutations · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A municipal search, accessibility, privacy, and security team. They need cross-signal metadata, WCAG-linked static findings, resource non-claims, controlled dynamic-service handoff, secure outputs, accessible remediation workflows, and adversarial native tests. Planned artifact: Deliver Accessible Secure Search and Site-Quality Report System with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Resolve canonical references and compare redirects, sitemap, internal links, content identity, reachability, and cycles.
Link findings to WCAG 2.2 criteria, affected users, page state, severity rationale, evidence, uncertainty, and human owner without conformance claims.
Separate static resource inventory, controlled browser lab metrics, field telemetry, accessibility effects, and production claims.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Canonical Graph
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go team handles case 5750 during a keyboard-first accessible report review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove original fixtures, RoundTrippers, httptest faults, fuzz and race invariants, and mutation detection; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Resolve canonical references and compare redirects, sitemap, internal links, content identity, reachability, and cycles.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Canonical Graph.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Mutation Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-mutation-evidence-te46os" that uses case 9014 to prove original fixtures, RoundTrippers, httptest faults, fuzz and race invariants, and mutation detection. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
